docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Cup with handle pattern was developed by William O Neil
  • One of the best chart patterns, Yet one of most misinterpreted
  • It's supposed to form during uptrend, it's basically 2 wave VCP
  • The Handle represent a shakeout where weak holders sell
  • It is preferable if the base is round and not in a V shape
  • A lot of cup with handle suggest beginning of bull market

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Double bottom Pattern is developed by William Jiler
  • Looks like lopsided W
  • It represent a fake downtrend signal that becomes corrected very quickly and leads to FOMO buying and stock moving up quickly
  • Mirroring the Market: Double bottoms tend to form while the overall market is volatile, and that's reflected in the shape
  • You have one down leg, then the stock tries to rally but hits resistance and ends up pulling back to form a second down leg
  • The stock rebounds one more time and is finally able to punch through and move higher
  • The breakout typically occurs when the overall market has also bounced back from a correction into a new uptrend

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• In earning breakout stock is making good breakout pattern before it's earning release
  • But breakout is not happen yet due to earning in next 4-5 days, lot of trader don't like to buy stock when earning is due

docs.title: When to enter
docs.text:
• Find low risk setup with volatility contraction
  • best time to buy is when quaterly results are annouced, stock becomes very volatile for first 5 min and can give very low risk entry
  • Use intraday time frame to take entry

docs.title: When to exit
docs.text:
• just use normal selling rules
  • If you have really large breakout after earning then sell at 2*ADR or close to any resistance zone like ATH line or previous swing high

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Flat base Pattern is developed by William Jiler
  • After the price has risen significantly in short period of time (mostly after breaking out into new high ground) then consolidate in tight area and create rectangle type fo formation. That is a flat base
  • In flatbase stock normally trade Sideways to "Digest" Earlier Gains
  • stocks will often break out of a cup with handle or double bottom pattern, run up at least 20%, then trade essentially sideways to form a flat base
  • Price range will usually remain fairly tight throught the base. which meean institutional investors who have to buy large positions are quietly buying withint a certain price range. that's how they increase their holding without increasing their price range
  • flat bases also have a way of shedding weaker holders. flat base shakeout is more of a slow grind. the weaker, less commited investors just get worn out by the indecisive, sideways action and eventually lose patience and sell.

docs.text: • on breakout of flat base

docs.title: What is it?
docs.text:
• An “inside bar” pattern is a two-bar price action trading strategy in which the inside bar is smaller and within the high to low range of the prior bar
  • the high is lower than the previous bar’s high, and the low is higher than the previous bar’s low
  • Its relative position can be at the top, the middle or the bottom of the prior bar.
  • The prior bar, the bar before the inside bar, is often referred to as the “mother bar”. You will sometimes see an inside bar referred to as an “ib” and its mother bar referred to as an “mb”.
  • it's Range contraction pattern for candles over 5-10 days

If i want to explain this in simple terms, it represent balance between buyer and sellers for that perticular day, so any movement above that bar will consider as bullish movement.

docs.title: When to buy?
docs.text:
• Prefer Range contraction with atleast 4-5 candles
  • we are mostly interested in late stage candles in range contraction
  • make sure that rate of change should not be more than 2.5%
  • Make sure that stock is consoidating at very low risk point
  • Make sure stock is close to 10 or 20 day ma
  • Put stoploss below Inside bar or below mother bar depend on price action

Note : Don't use inside bar as setup, use it in combination with other setups to take early entry or building positions or pyramid.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Average Daily Range (ADR) is a technical indicator that measures the average price range of a stock over a specific period of time, I most look back up to 20 trading days to calculate the ADR.
  • It's good indicator of how fast stock can go up or down
  • High ADR% is good indicator if stock is capable of showing strong momentum or not
  • A low ADR stock will give you favorable move but it will crawl like snail while high ADR stock will fly like a bird

docs.title: Calculation
docs.text:
• The ADR is calculated by taking the difference between the high and low price of each trading day, summing these differences over the specified period, and then dividing by the number of trading days in that period.
  • For example, if we are calculating the ADR over a 20-day period, we would take the high and low price of each of the 20 trading days, calculate the difference for each day, sum these differences, and then divide by 20 to get the average daily range.
  • The formula for ADR can be expressed as: 
    ADR = (Sum of (High - Low) for each trading day) / Number of trading days

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
• A higher ADR indicates that the stock has more volatility and is likely to experience larger price movements, which can provide better trading opportunities.
  • A lower ADR indicates that the stock has less volatility and may not provide as many trading opportunities, especially for short-term traders who rely on price movements to make profits.
  • When selecting stocks for trading, I prefer to look for stocks with an ADR of 3% or higher, as these stocks are more likely to provide good trading opportunities due to their higher volatility.
  • However, it's important to also consider other factors such as the overall market conditions, the stock's trend, and its liquidity when making trading decisions, as a high ADR alone does not guarantee a successful trade.

Warning

Don't think ADR to be kind of assurance that stock will only go up, if it has the potential to go up quickly, it has potential to come down even quicker.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Free float is the number of shares of a stock that are available for trading in the market
  • It is calculated by subtracting the number of shares held by promoters, insiders, and other restricted shareholders from the total number of shares outstanding
  • Free float value is a measure of the liquidity and market capitalization of a stock.
  • A higher free float value indicates that there are more shares available for trading, which can lead to better price discovery and less volatility.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• GTT (Good till trigger) allows you to put buy and sell order on kite
  • It has 1 year expiry date so it's good enough for cash trading
  • You can put order anytime you want

docs.title: Using GTT for Buying and Selling
docs.text:
• This is original purpose of GTT to buy and sell automatically based on trigger value
  • This allows you to keep your eyes away from market and focus on work after buying selling is done
  • There's multiple type of GTT orders but Most simple buy/sell order is most usefull for cash trading
  • You can put multiple buy order even if you don't have money so which ever order got triggered first that will get executed
  • Just put buy order when stock is about to breakout

When you place any buy order using gtt make sure to keep some extra funds for taxes or brokerage charges, without that order won't get executed

docs.title: Using GTT for alerts
docs.text:
• I am using simple GTT buy order with 1 quantity and 1rs price as alerts, it gtt will get triggerd order will get rejected due to stock price lower than stock value
  • Kite has seprate feature for putting alerts but one thing i dislike about alert is that it's hard to figure out which alerts are about to be triggered
  • GTT display percentages from your trigger point, so it's easy to see which stock are close to breakout point
  • This is really good advantage, you can review stock just before breakout and place buy order if it's really good
  • Stocks moves really fast after breakout point so you can buy it at minimal risk reducing slippage

docs.title: GTT Csv File
docs.text:
• Kite allows you to download GTT data in csv format
  • So you can use that csv file for multiple things
    • Ignore GTT stocks in screener
    • get Stoploss from all stocks in Investing log
    • get list of your priority stocks with trigger point in excel

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• An inside bar is a candlestick pattern where the current bar's high and low are completely contained within the previous bar's high and low.
  • It indicates a period of consolidation and indecision in the market, often preceding a breakout in either direction.
  • it's basically volatility contraction on smaller time frame

docs.title: How to Trade Inside Bar
docs.text:
• Identify an inside bar pattern on the chart, where the current bar is completely contained within the previous bar.
  • Wait for a breakout from the inside bar pattern, which can occur in either direction (upward or downward).
  • If the price breaks above the high of the inside bar, it may signal a bullish breakout
  • If the price breaks below the low of the inside bar, it may signal a bearish breakout
  • Consider using additional technical indicators or chart patterns to confirm the breakout and increase the probability of a successful trade.
  • As shown in below image there's no correct way to enter
  • all entry points are trade off between capturing a move with a big size vs getting shaken out, or even entring in a move that was never there

docs.title: NSE Percentage of Stocks
docs.text:
• NSE Percentage of Stocks is a market breadth indicator that measures the percentage of stocks in the Nifty 50 index that are trading above their 10/20/50/200-day moving average.

  • Sample Data for Percentage of Stock above 10,20 and 50 Day Moving Average

    Date 10 20 50 17 May 64.49 56.8 54.95 16 May 54.26 50.31 49.23 15 May 50.85 47.41 47.77 14 May 43.38 40.45 44.92 13 May 24.31 27.87 37.49 10 May 21.25 27.52 38.50 9 May 14.43 21.77 34.96 8 May 28.89 38.48 47.10 7 May 22.82 33.18 44.62 6 May 35.16 47.21 53.71 3 May 49.08 60.56 62.00 2 May 60.74 67.35 65.40

  • I am only interested in percentage of stock 10,20,50 day

  • I mostly look at 10 and 20 day percentage value to figure out daily trend. 50 day is trend confirmation

  • This table explains how to interpret percentage of stock above 10/20/50 day moving average and what action to take based on it.

    Percentage Market 0-20 Oversold 20-50 Weak market 50-80 Healthy Market 80-100 Overbought Market

  • When Should I start Buying

    Above 50% Action 10 25% Market Exposure 21 50% market exposure 50 Go all in

  • When shoud i start selling

    below 55% Action 10 No New Buy, Start Booking Profits, basically sell anything below 10EMA or low profit margin 20 Sell everything without profit margin 50 Sell everything and move to cash

  • From Sample data you can clearly identify when market starting get weak from 2-3 may to 6-7 may

  • This is my current favorite method to adjust my market exposure, this is much more reliable than just watching index

Tip

It's hard to pin point exact moment when market turn bad, so just use your best judgement. Also when market look bad don't focus on risk reward when booking profits just sell, don't try to be perfect.

docs.title: Net New Highs and Lows
docs.text:
• Displays the net number of stocks on NSE (India) making 52-week highs or lows. The count is displayed as a fraction of the total number of stocks in the NSE universe.
  • Net New Highs (NNH) are calculated by taking the number of new 52-week highs on any given day and subtracting from it the number of new 52-week lows.
  • A market is considered strong (bullish) when NNH are positive, or, in other words, when new highs exceed new lows.
  • We consider the bias as positive (bullish) when NNH stays positive for 3 consecutive days, & negative (bullish) when NNH stays negative for 3 consecutive days.

docs.title: Gap ups
docs.text:
• If stock that is about to breakout, breakout with gap up then just wait for first few min and let stock make it's first base on smaller time frame
  • Most time stock will gap up in pre market and then some selling will happen, then it will again start moving up breaking open price then you can enter, with day's low as SL

docs.title: Gap downs
docs.text:
• Most gap downs is going to happen due to some negative news like bad earnings, change in gov policy
  • For earnign just never hold anything going through earnings
  • For other reasons just sell on smaller time frame

docs.title: Black swan events
docs.text:
• When there's major change in market expections black swan event will happen
  • Most of time it's negative news
  • Some examples of black swan events in past few years
    • Global pandemic like covid 2020
    • War Russia-Ukraine war, iran-isrel , isrel-hamas
    • Major Shift in National Government, Lok sabha election 2024, BJP didn't got majority
    • Major economical bubble like Housing market bubble, IT companies bubble
  • If you're in cash
    • can buy good companies in uptrend with good relative strength for short term hold
    • Focus on companies which is still above 10/21 day with low volume selling
    • If you really have high conviction on company then only buy it on same day otherwise buy it on next day rebounce
    • only buy 1-2 positions and watch it like hawk
  • If you're invested
    • Just start booking profits and move to cash, don't wait for market close

docs.title: Market crash
docs.text:
• Market crash is going to happen when there is some major negative news like war, recession, pandemic
  • In market crash just stay away from market and wait for few days to see how market is reacting
  • If you're already invested then just make decision based on your risk appetite
    • Sell if you're sure that market is going to crash more and things are going to get worse
    • Hold if you're not sure or you think that market is going to recover soon in next few days

docs.title: Candle without Wig
docs.text: - For buying if stock open with gap up and keep going up with very small wick it's very bullish action and take immediate entry - Similarly for selling if stock gap up and keep going not even making any wick on top it's good sign of selling pressure, take immediate exit to protect your profit - You can use this concept for early selling or buying for swing trading.

docs.title: Swing Trading
docs.text:
• When you buy stock for shorter duration just few days
  • Low risk stoploss withing 2-4%
  • you normally ride stock until first momentum burst
  • If you find any good uptrending stock you'll find multiple swing trading opportunity in single stock
  • stoploss is very tight, day low or low of past few day or 10 day depending on price action
  • You normally sit out before earnings
  • Require less skill, you won't loose much even after making mistakes
  • Low Risk setup are more suited for this
    • Base on base breakout
    • Inside bar breakout
    • Flag breakouts after big base breakout in uptrending stocks
    • Range or trend breakout with clear pivot point and low risk entry
    • more suited for beginners with small to medium amount of capital (less than 1 crore)
  • Benefits
    • You're benifit greatly with high number of trades
    • much higher level of compounding if you manage your position well
    • You'll be out of market at very small loss when market health deteriorite or market crash
    • you can do this in any market condition, even in bear market there's lot of small rallies for few weeks, same case for side ways market
    • since you're taking more number of trades for small duration, stock picking is not that critical. you'll have time to correct your mistakes
  • Downside
    • You can miss out on un expected gains due to market news or good news about stock like very strong earnings
    • Very high maintainace, you'll be taking positions or selling out on almost every day so expect 3-4 hours of active trading
    • Sometimes stocks can keep going up without any good setups or market health
    • not much to doo when market is not uptrending or very volatile

docs.title: Positional Trading
docs.text:
• When you buy stock for longer span
  • Stoploss is high for this kind of trading around 6-8%
  • you can ride stock until next base or for entire duration of stage 2 uptrend
  • if you find any uptrending stock you can keep builing position after each base/pivot
  • normally sit through earning release volatility
  • stoploss is below 3-4% below 21 day or last swing low
  • More benificial with large capital individuals (in crore), because it's easly to build large position with this method and you don't have to spend entire day on your computer
  • require more skills for right execution and stock picking
  • Base breakouts are most suited for this
    • VCP, cup with handle, flat base, Double bottom, Ipo base, retest ath
    • low cheat setup that i am currently using
    • big base ath breakout
    • big Flag breakout after stock is up more than 50-70% in previous uptrend
  • Benefits
    • low maintainace system, can be done with full time job
    • you'll be benifit a lot during short correction 5-10%, you'll losse some money but you'll regain your profit in next 3-4 days nothing to about
  • Downside
    • Good stock selection is very important part for positional trading. if you pick bad stock or bad sector you'll miss out on atleast 1 rally
    • Since you're not watching market entire day, you can missout on selling during market crash or market health deteriorate.
    • During major carsh Lossing major chuck of profit withing 3-4 days can be really bad for your mental health
    • Major gap down due to some bad news can wipe out your months worth of gain
    • Only suitable for full bull market, you'll sit out in cash during bear market

Tip

Do swing trading to build up your capital since you have more time in early years and can take more risk. switch to positional trading when you have expertise and big capital

docs.title: How Pine Scripts are Executed?
docs.text:
• A pine script is not like programs in many programming languages that executes once and then stop
  • it's runtime environment, a script runs in the equivalent of an invisible loop where it is executed once on each bar of whatever chart you are on, from left to right
  • Chart bars that ave already closed when the script executes on them are called historical bars. when execution reaches the chart's last bar adn the market is open, it is on the realtime bar.
  • The script then executes once every tiem a price or volume change is detected, and one last time for that realtime bar when it closes. That realtime bar then becomes elapsed time bar. Note that when the script ececutes in realtime, it does not recalculate on all the chart's historical bars on every price/volume update. It has already calculated once on those bars, so it does not need to recalculate then on every chart tick.

docs.title: Time series
docs.text:
• the main data strcuture used in pine script is callled a time series
  • Time series contain one value for each bar the script executes on, so they continuously expand as the script executes on more bars.
  • For example for closing value of last bar use close variable, but if you need previous bar closing value you need to use close[1], last bar will have 0 index

docs.title: Events that trigger execution of script
docs.text:
• For complete bars
    • A new symbol or timeframe is loaded on a chart
    • A script is saved or added to chart
    • A value is modified in script settings
    • a browser refresh
  • for realtime bar
    • all events of complete bars also applies here
    • when there a change in price or volume detected

docs.title: Script
docs.text:
//@version=5
indicator("Test",overlay = true)
//Inputs
horizontalLineLocation=input(2000,"Horizontal Line Location")
//Plot Horizontal Line to Existing Chart
hline(horizontalLineLocation)
//Plot line connecting closing price of chart
closingPlot=plot(close,color = color.red)//close is closing price and open is for open price
openingPlot=plot(open,color = color.green)//close is closing price and open is for open price
//Fill part between two plot
fill(openingPlot,closingPlot,color = color.white)
- //@version=5 - This is a compiler annotation telling the compiler the script will use version 5 of Pine Script. - indicator("Test",overlay = true) - Defines the name of the script that will appear on the chart as “Test”. - overlay (const bool) If true, the indicator will be displayed over the chart. If false, it will be added in a separate pane. Optional. The default is false. - horizontalLineLocation=input(2000,"Horizontal Line Location") - Adds an input to the Inputs tab of your script's Settings, with default value - hline(horizontalLineLocation) - Plot Horizontal Line with 2000 Yaxis value to Existing Chart - closingPlot=plot(close,color = color.red) - Plot graph using closing price - fill(openingPlot,closingPlot,color = color.white) - Fill Part between two plots

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Pine Seeds is a service to import your custom data and access it via TradingView. This service allows you to: - connect your series data to TradingView - open it on the TradingView chart - use it in the custom indicators

refer: PineSeed Docs

Use TradingView as your frontend and use a GitHub repository as your backend.

Keep in mind that such data (we call them EOD data, short for End-of-Day) has certain limitations:

  • the data can only be updated 5 times per day
  • only daily-based timeframes (1D and above) can be applied to such data
  • the number of data elements (symbols) is limited to 6000
  • such data will not appear in the symbol search box

Setting up the service includes several steps:

  1. Setting up a repository.
  2. Data preparation.
  3. Manipulating data in the TradingView UI.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
You can take the seed_crypto_santiment project as a reference for your repository. The project has the proper structure for symbol data.

You can also open symbols from seed_crypto_santiment on the TradingView chart and work with it. For example, SEED_CRYPTO_SANTIMENT:BTC_DEV_ACTIVITY is an example of custom data integration. The symbol name is uniquely determined by the GitHub settings.

  • SEED is a required prefix.
  • CRYPTO is the account name github.com/crypto.
  • SANTIMENT is the suffix of the GitHib repository name github.com/crypto/seed_crypto_santiment. The suffix is needed so that you can use several repositories for different data groups, e.g., seed_<username>_<suffix1>, seed_<username>_<suffix2>.
  • BTC_DEV_ACTIVITY is the BTC_DEV_ACTIVITY.CSV data file name.

Using the built-in request.seed() function from the Pine Script™ language and the available data, you can build a chart.

//@version=5
indicator("BTC Dev Activity", format=format.volume)
//request.seed(source, symbol, expression)
activity = request.seed("seed_crypto_santiment", "BTC_DEV_ACTIVITY", close)
plot(activity, "BTC Dev Activity")

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: • Before we start trading, it's crucial to analyze the overall market conditions to check if the market is in a favorable state for trading.

docs.title: Index Analysis
docs.text:
• We need to make sure that major indices like Nifty and Smallcap index are in an uptrend and showing strength.
  • Moving averages for 10/21/50 must be in the correct order (10 > 21 > 50) and the price should be above these moving averages to confirm a strong uptrend.
  • We should also look for signs of strength in the indices, such as higher highs and higher lows, to confirm that the market is in a bullish phase.

docs.title: Market Breadth
docs.text:
• Market breadth must be above 50% for 10/21 Day at start of uptrend
  • All 10/21/50 day breadth must be above 80% for healthy market conditions.

docs.title: Net new Highs vs Lows
docs.text: • Must be positive for last 3-4 days to confirm that the market is in a strong uptrend.

docs.title: How to trade in different market conditions
Market is in a strong uptrend
docs.text:
• Just do normal Swing trading
  • Use leverage if you're confident in market conditions

docs.title: Market is oversold
docs.text:
• Focus on buying Nifty or Smallcap index and avoid trading individual stocks
  • Only Do this when market is at a strong support level like 200 day moving average or vwap support
  • This is mostly folloed by long term downtrend and one big down day due to some bad news or event
  • Don't be scared to buy index in such conditions as they are mostly short term and will recover in 1-2 weeks

docs.title: Market is in a downtrend
docs.text:
• Avoid trading and stay on the sidelines and just monitor
  • If market looks oversold and index is over extended from it's 10/21/50 day moving averages we can look for short term index buying opportunities with tight stop loss

docs.title: Market is in a choppy range
docs.text:
• Only do low risk(2-3% stoploss) trading with tight stop loss half position side
  • Try to buy before breakout during morning or after 3:00 PM
  • Don't hold for long term and do profit booking as soon as stock looks extended from 10 day moving average

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Stock screening is the process of filtering stocks based on specific criteria to identify potential investment opportunities. It helps investors narrow down the vast universe of stocks to a manageable list that meets their investment goals and risk tolerance.
  • I have developed two method for Stock Screening
    • Trading view screener good for live scans
    • Excel screener good for daily or weekly scans
  • Run Screener everyday to figure out new breakout or to catch good looking setups early

docs.title: Selection Criteria for Stock Screening
docs.text:
• You
  • Exchange
    • We will only focus on stocks listed on the NSE due to better liquidity more regulation
    • BSE stocks can be more volatile and less liquid only consider if it's a very promising stock that is not listed on NSE
  • Market Cap
    • Range : 500cr - 2,00,000cr
    • We are looking for mid-cap and small-cap stocks that have the potential for significant growth
    • Large-cap stocks are generally more stable but may not offer the same growth opportunities
  • Price
    • Range : 30 - 7000
    • We want to avoid penny stocks (below 30) which can be highly volatile and risky
    • We also want to avoid very high-priced stocks (above 7000) that we can't buy in significant quantities with our budget plus avoid liquidity issues that can arise with very high-priced stocks
  • EMA 21
    • Only consider stocks that are trading above their 21-day Exponential Moving Average (EMA) to ensure we are looking at stocks in an uptrend
  • All time High
    • Only consider stocks that are within 20% of their all-time high to ensure we are looking at stocks with strong momentum and potential for further growth
  • Price change %
    • Only consider stock with a price change of -2% or more today to identify stocks that may be experiencing a temporary pullback and could be good buying opportunities
    • You can remove this criteria if you're doing weekly screening as it may not be relevant for longer-term analysis

docs.title: IPO Screener
docs.text:
• Vivek IPO Screener
  • Market Cap > 300cr
  • Price > 30
  • Stock must be listed 12 month ago or less

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Now that we have list of stock from scanner, we need to filter them manually to find the best ones to trade.
  • This Criteria need to check manually by looking at the charts and can't be automated.
  • Current market conditions must be taken into account when applying these criteria

docs.title: Positive Criteria (Must have)
docs.text:
• Stock must be in Stage 2 Uptrend
  • Free Float Value >= 300 Cr
    • By setting a minimum free float value of 300 Cr, we can ensure that we are trading stocks that have sufficient liquidity and are less likely to be manipulated by large investors.
  • Stock must be within 15-20% of All Time High
    • less overhead supply and more likely to have strong momentum
  • Stock Should belong to trending sector
    • Prioritize stocks that belong to sectors that are currently trending in the market.
    • Trending sectors are more likely to experience strong price movements and can provide better trading opportunities.
    • Average looking stock from trending sector is better than stock with good technicals from downtrending sector
  • Volume contraction with good volume on breakout day
    • Volume contraction indicates that the stock is consolidating and building up energy for a potential breakout.
    • A breakout with good volume confirms that there is strong buying interest and increases the likelihood of a sustained uptrend.
  • Low Risk Stoploss
    • A low stoploss allows us to limit our potential losses if the trade doesn't go as planned.
    • Preferably within 8% of the entry price
  • EMA 10 > EMA 21 > EMA 50
    • This moving average configuration indicates a strong uptrend and can help us identify stocks that are likely to continue moving higher.

docs.title: Optional Positive Criteria (Nice to have)
docs.text:
• Breakout Chart pattern
    • A breakout chart pattern, such as a cup and handle, ascending triangle, or flag pattern, can provide additional confirmation of a potential uptrend.
    • More easy to identify and trade compared to stocks without a clear chart pattern
  • Stock with good story or theme
    • A stock with a good story or theme can attract more investors and create positive sentiment around the stock, which can contribute to its price appreciation.
  • Prefer stock with atleast 1 base count
    • Stock with 1 breakout from a base make sure that you don't have to wait for a long time for next uptrend to start
  • Good Relative Strength
    • A stock with good relative strength is outperforming the overall market and can be a strong candidate for trading.
    • Compare stock with MIDSmall400 index to calculate relative strength
    • Relative strength chart must be in short term uptrend
    • Not required but if relative strength is breaking out of downtrend then it's very positive sign
  • ADR Should be in range of 3-5% or more
    • A stock with an ADR of 3-5% or more indicates that it has sufficient volatility to provide good trading opportunities.
  • Fundamenetal Checks on market smith
    • Price strength rating > 70
    • Group rank < 50, but prefered <30
    • Stock Quartry earning growth should me more than 20%
    • Survellance check should be greed, ignore asm list warning
  • Fundamentals on screener for related stocks
    • Check sector and industy in peer comparison
    • If possible find out related stock with similar chart patterns
    • you should be able to figure out if entire group is breakign out

docs.title: Negative Criteria (Avoid at all costs)
docs.text:
• Avoid stock with 2-5% circuit limit
    • Stocks with a 5% circuit limit can be highly volatile and risky also very low chance of significant price movement in a single day which can limit our ability to capitalize on short-term opportunities
    • Additioinnally, stocks with a 5% circuit limit may be more susceptible to manipulation and we don't want to get caught in a situation where we can't sell our shares due to a circuit limit being hit
  • Avoid stocks with low liquidity
    • Need more Details here
  • Avoid FNO stocks
    • FNO stocks can be more volatile and risky due to the presence of derivatives trading, which can amplify price movements and increase the potential for losses.
  • Avoid stocks with bad price action
    • Stock not following the expected price pattern or showing signs of weakness can be a red flag
  • Avoid stock which is close to earning date (3-5 day)
    • Earnings announcements can lead to increased volatility and unpredictable price movements, which can make it difficult to manage risk effectively.

docs.title: Optional Negative Criteria (Should Avoid if possible)
docs.text:
• Avoid stock with high base count > 5
    • Stock with a high base count can be more volatile
    • Low chances of large move
  • Bad Financials
    • Stock with Bad EPS or bad revenue growth

docs.title: Additional Tips from personal Experience
docs.text:
• You don't have to keep buying new stocks for each setup, normally after correction stock that broke out, it keep making smaller base and keep moving up. So keep eye on missed breakout too. You will get multiple swing trading opportunity in uptrending stocks.
  • Trading same stock is simpler than buying new stocks every week because you'll be mroe aware about that perticular stock, it's earnings, price action.
  • Set right expectations. Understand different between large base ath breakout and pivot point breakout. with large base breakout you can expect 20-30% move before consolidation and for pivot breakout(flag,pennant) expect only 15-20% upmove.

docs.title: Position Sizing
docs.text:
• Goal of position sizing is to manage risk and maximize returns by determining the appropriate amount of capital to allocate to each trade based on the trader's risk tolerance and the specific trade setup.
  • General conviction is to risk only 1% of capital on each trade
  • You can adjust this percentage based on number positions you want to keep open at a time | Position % |Risk|Stocks| | -------- | ------- | ------- | | 100 |1% | 1 | | 50 |2% | 2 | | 25 |4% | 4 | | 12.5 |8% | 8 |

docs.title: General Tips
docs.text:
• Try to Keep your watchlist as clean as possible, don't add too many stocks to watchlist, if you have too many stocks to watch try to trimdown list to 10-15 stocks at max
  • Buy stock within 1-2% of breakout point, don't buy extended stocks
  • Your stoploss should not be more than 5-6% for any stock
  • Mindfull of risk reward ratio, Higher stop-loss also mean low risk reward ration so choose stock with lower risk
  • Use Trading view app instead of browser for multiple display, so you can keep eye on index and stocks at same time
  • Use Trading view alerts to keep track of breakout on intraday timeframe, that's your buy/sell signal
  • Keep working on 5min chart breakout database to train you eyes on intraday buying

docs.title: Before market opens
docs.text:
• Create separate list for ready to buy stocks
  • Setup kite alert for these stocks

docs.title: During Pre-market
docs.text: • If stock is about to breakout that you can place GTT order on live market

docs.title: Live Market
docs.text:
• If you have placed GTT order then you can just wait for it to get executed
  • Try to buy stock before it breakout, so you can get good risk reward ratio, but if you miss that then it's better to wait for stock to breakout and then buy on pullback
  • Don't do fomo buying, if you miss one breakout then there will be another breakout in next few hours, so be patient and wait for good setup

docs.title: Why Intraday for buying?
docs.text:
• Because when stock is just about to breakout and close to 10EMA, at morning we can take very low risk entry in stock with 2-3% of stoploss
  • when stock breakout after this entry and at day end it closes with 3-4% profit then we can carry that position for swing trade or positional trade for next few days
  • when stock breakout didn't sustain then sometime we'll exit at small loss/small profit and it's okay
  • Earning gains, sometime stocks is just close to breakout but everyone is waiting for earning results, you'll start buying when results is out and stock is start to going up, it's normally very quick trade good gain in 1-2 day

docs.title: Things to take care when buying on intraday
docs.text:
• First 15 mins are normally very volatile and chances of stock sellout or reversal is very high so be causius only buy in first 5 min when market health is really good and is uptrending other wise you wait for small base on 5 min chart so you can manage your losses
  • It's okay to miss out on trades, you'll find multiple breakout in first hours, so best idea to only buy low risk stocks don't do fomo buying

docs.title: When to sell on intraday
docs.text:
• when at end of day you don't have good profit cusion on your stock and market condition is not that good then it's good idea to close that trade
  • when your stock make big gain after buying then looses most of it's gain basically squat down, then it's good idea to sell half/full depending on market condition

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Always use limit order for selling, don't ever use market order, except for emergency
  • Use GTT SL for everything, never trade without Stoploss
  • Don't wait till EOD to sell, Sell as soon as stock hit your stoploss, If stock recover well with good volume you can buy it back but if stock didn't recover then you will lost another 2-5% beyound your stoploss which will affect your stats

docs.title: Initial Stoploss
docs.text:
• Use Previous Swing Low or day's low depending on setup and market
  • Move to trailing stoploss when 10/21 Day moving average is above your stoploss

Tip

When you take entry in morning make sure that you have 2-3% of profit cushion by end of day. If you don't have profit cushion by end of day or stock have weak closing then it's better to sell full/half with small loss.

docs.title: Trailing Stoploss
docs.text:
• When Market is strong
    • Use 10/21 Day MA as stoploss but give 3-4% extra space below MA to deal with shakeouts
    • Some stocks follow 10D MA and some follow 21D MA, so use your personal instinct for this
  • When Market is weak and you're looking to sell
    • Use previous day low as trailing stoploss

docs.title: Stoploss based on Market Conditions
docs.text:
• this is like sub category of stoploss
  • Current market aware ness is also taken into consideration for this
  • when market is overbought and you're expecting pull back swich to sell stoploss
  • when market is oversold but there's still lot of volatility then stick with hard stoploss even when you're in good profit
  • Hard Stoploss
    • When i don't want to chock my trade but want to protect my capital from worst case scenarios
    • Hard stoploss should not trigger for daily price volatility
    • normally taken considering price action of day chart
  • Sell Stoploss
    • when i've already decided to take exit due to negative price action or want to protect major chuch of my profit
    • soft stoploss will trigger when there's sudden fall or high volatility
    • normally taken on smaller time frame 5/15 min or on previous day's low

docs.title: Selling Process depending on different scenarios
When you buy stock breaking out of base
docs.text:
• VCP, CWH are base breakout patterns
  • Use Swing low as intial SL
  • Trail your stoploss, don't choke your trade

docs.title: When you buy Uptrending stock
docs.text:
• bull flag, bull pennant, high tight flag, flat base are continuation pattern
  • For this type of brakout don't wait for stock to break down, Just sell into strength when stock looks extended, or use tight stoploss to lock in profit

docs.title: When Stock is extended
docs.text:
• Move your SL to extended day low or 10Day whichever is higher
  • Don't chock your trade, stock can go extended for multiple days
  • you can also book 50% if you're holding very large position to reduce risk.
  • For regular position don't sell half, because it will increase number of stocks in your portfolio

docs.title: When there's change in circuit limit
docs.text:
• When there's change in circuit limit and stock is extended then it's good idea to sell and book profit
  • Change in circuit limit mostly put limit on stock upside and people will move to other stock with more upside potential

docs.title: When Earning Results is due
docs.text:
• Never hold a stock through earnings if you don't have enough cushion to protect against potential downside.
  • When Market is strong
    • if you have 10%+ profit cushion and related industry group performance is also strong then and then only hold through earning
    • If stocks within the same industry group have generally reacted well to earnings, it may potentially be an indicator that it is safer to hold through earnings.
  • When Market is Weak
    • Always sell 1 day before earning
    • Buy back if results are good and Market response is positive

Warning

earnings events are binary events and there are no guarantees. You must be willing to give up potential gains or face losses.

docs.title: When Market is weak
docs.text:
• When index is in short downtrend
  • When market breath is looking very weak
  • then it's good idea to tighen your stoploss and lock in some profit

docs.title: When you buy stock in morning and have weak closing
docs.text:
• first check underlying index for that stock, if it's looking good no need to do anything
  • if not you need to limit your losses by either reducing position size or keep tight stoploss for next day. you can set fix 1-2% stoploss or set stoploss at previous 5 min swing low

docs.title: When Stock squat after big gain
docs.text: • Sell half if stock loose most of it's gain, sell full if it goes below day's low

docs.title: NSE Percentage of Stocks
docs.text:
• https://in.tradingview.com/script/qQ04TGdJ-NSE-Percentage-of-stocks-above-Moving-Averages/
  • This indicator displays the percentage of NSE (India) stocks trading above key moving averages.
  • Market breadth measures the degree of participation & the conviction in the overall mood of the underlying index
  • A positive market breadth is said to happen when more stocks are advancing than are declining
  • When most of the stocks are trading above a specific moving average, the market breadth is termed strong.
  • How to Interprete Results
  • We have a bullish bias when >50% of stocks are above their 50-day and 200-day MAs. We have a bearish bias when <50% of stocks are above their 50-day and 200-day MAs.
  • We also look at short-term timeframes (10 & 20 MA) for overbought and oversold levels. Values above 80% are considered overbought and readings below 20% are deemed oversold.
  • Oversold/overbrought market breadth does not necessarily indicate reversal, but rather an exhaustion. This can get resolved by either a price correction or a time correction.
  • The breadth can remain in overbought zones for a long time while the price is in a strong uptrend — and equally so at oversold zones during a strong downtrend.

docs.title: NSE Net New Highs
docs.text:
• https://in.tradingview.com/script/W433ezuL-NSE-Net-New-Highs/
  • This indicator displays the net number of stocks on NSE (India) making 52-week highs or lows. The count is displayed as a fraction of the total number of stocks in the NSE universe.
  • Net New Highs (NNH) are calculated by taking the number of new 52-week highs on any given day and subtracting from it the number of new 52-week lows.
  • A market is considered strong (bullish) when NNH are positive, or, in other words, when new highs exceed new lows.

docs.title: Simple Volume with Pocket Pivots
docs.text:
• https://in.tradingview.com/script/JkB0iCFp-Simple-Volume-with-Pocket-Pivots/
  • Display volume in seperate pane with some extra features
  • Blue bars: Pocket Pivot Volumes (PPV), that is an up-day volume which is greater than the highest down-day volume of the last 10 down-days.
  • Red bars: Down-day volumes that are greater than 50-period moving average of the volume.
  • Green bars: Up-day volumes that are greater than 50-period moving average of the volume.
  • Orange bars: Low/Dry volume bars. these are visible the volume has fallen to at least 1/5 from the 50-period moving average of the volume. You have the choice to alter this period to one of your choosing by changing the number used as "Low volume fraction”.
  • Gray Bars: All other volumes are “noise” & need not be kept into any actionable consideration.
  • Bull short volume are display using up arrow
